schema.name: NECTYR
schema.description: Secure web application for monitoring and managing Taypro Solar Panel Cleaning Robot fleets: dashboards, weather context, schedules, cleaning logs and exports, statistics, gateway health, controlled robot commands, fleet tracking, support tickets, and role-based user management.

hero.title: NECTYR, Solar Panel Cleaning Robot Fleet Portal
hero.subtitle: The secure web command centre for your Taypro robot fleet, live dashboards, weather-aware context, schedules, cleaning logs, exportable reports, analytics, and support workflows. Login details and the customer portal link are shared privately by Taypro after contract onboarding.

intro.eyebrow: Fleet software for robotic solar cleaning
intro.title: What is NECTYR?
intro.p1After: behind every major Taypro deployment. It translates raw robot telemetry into decisions plant teams actually use: when to clean, which blocks finished first, which assets need a closer look, and how last night's run compared to the monthly cleaning plan.
intro.p2After: , multi-site portfolios, multi-block plants, and mixed fleets of

capabilities.title: What your teams can do in NECTYR
capabilities.subtitle: High-level capability areas, your Taypro onboarding pack and user guide explain each screen in the depth your operators need.

0.title: Site dashboards
0.body: At-a-glance view of weather context, block-wise cleaning coverage, gateway connectivity, and robot battery readiness so operators can plan shifts with confidence.

1.title: Robot configuration & device layout
1.body: Structured visibility into deployed robots, block allocation, and permitted operating parameters, tuned by Taypro during commissioning, then monitored by your team.

2.title: Automation schedules
2.body: Centralised view of block-wise timers and cleaning windows so night-time or post-production cycles stay aligned with plant rules and seasonal soiling patterns.

3.title: Cleaning logs & exports
3.body: Historical cleaning activity, downloadable reports for selected dates, and tabbed views for completed runs, in-progress work, errors, and offline assets, built for audit-ready O&M.

4.title: Statistics & trends
4.body: Charts for area cleaned, blocks serviced, execution mix, and efficiency indicators, filterable by site, time range, and (where licensed) robot or block.

5.title: Controlled robot commands
5.body: A curated, permissioned command set for day-to-day operations, always confirmed, always logged. Deep engineering actions remain with Taypro service engineers.

6.title: Fleet tracking context
6.body: Live and last-known robot positioning on site layouts where available, ideal for coordination with security, O&M convoys, and incident response.

7.title: Gateway & field telemetry health
7.body: Visibility into gateway and field-device status so you can distinguish a true robot fault from a communications brownout before dispatching crews.

8.title: Tickets & service history
8.body: Raise, comment on, and close Taypro service tickets with a full timeline, fewer ad-hoc email threads, more traceability for your internal governance.

9.title: Users, roles & collaboration
9.body: Invite the right mix of plant, central engineering, and finance stakeholders with scoped access. Optional chat channels keep operational conversations next to the data.

security.title: Security, roles & auditability, without publishing our playbook
security.subtitle: NECTYR is built for critical infrastructure customers: authenticated access, role-based permissions, encrypted transport, session safeguards, and tamper-evident activity history for privileged actions. Exact control matrices evolve with each software release, your security questionnaire receives precise answers under NDA from Taypro IT.
security.card0Body: Client admins invite only the stakeholders who need a given site or block, permissions can be tightened or revoked instantly.
security.card1Body: Dashboards surface the same environmental context your robots use so human and machine decisions stay aligned.
security.card2Body: Tickets, chat, and logs live next to telemetry so Taypro support sees what your operators see, faster root cause, fewer loops.

workflow.title: How NECTYR fits into your solar O&M workflow
workflow.p1: NECTYR is not a generic plant SCADA, and it is not a replacement for your work-management system. It is a focused fleet layer for robotic solar panel cleaning that sits alongside the tools your operations team already runs, plant SCADA for generation, CMMS for tickets and assets, and your field reporting stack for daily logs.
workflow.p2Before: Cleaning crews use NECTYR to confirm last night's block-wise coverage before the morning shift. Plant managers use it to compare planned vs. actual cycles against the monthly cleaning strategy. Central engineering uses it to spot soiling trends across multiple sites in a portfolio. Finance teams use the exports to reconcile cleaning evidence with O&M billing, especially useful for plants on
workflow.p2After: where billing is per panel cleaned.
workflow.p3Before: If you're still deciding between owning a robot fleet and consuming cleaning as a service, our
workflow.categoryLink: Solar Panel Cleaning Robot category page
workflow.p3After: walks through GLYDE, HELYX, GLYDE-X, and OPEX side by side, NECTYR is the operations layer behind every one of those paths.
workflow.blogAnalytics: data analytics in solar cleaning operations

scheduling.title: Schedules, rest zones, and cleaning windows, explained
scheduling.p1: Robotic solar cleaning at utility scale is not about pressing "clean now". The hard part is choosing how often, in which order, and inside which environmental window. NECTYR exposes the planning loop that Taypro engineers and your operators agree on during commissioning, so cleaning cadence stays consistent week after week, season after season.
scheduling.li0: Each block carries its own cleaning window, typically post-sunset or pre-sunrise, so cleaning never overlaps peak generation.
scheduling.li1: The portal shows where each robot is meant to idle between cycles. Site teams use the same map for security rounds and morning checks.
scheduling.li2: Plants in dusty zones move to denser cycles (often 6–10 per month) during summer and post-harvest; quieter months drop to 3–4 cycles. NECTYR keeps the working schedule visible to everyone on the account.
scheduling.li3: Dashboards surface the same weather signal robots use to skip wet or windy cycles, so manual overrides happen with full context.
scheduling.p2Before: Want a quick sense of how cycle cadence translates to generation gain and payback for your plant? Run the numbers in the
scheduling.roiLink: Solar Panel Cleaning Robot ROI calculator

visual.title: From fleet overview to per-robot clarity
visual.subtitle: Search across sites, jump into a single robot context, compare blocks, and export evidence, without losing the thread of what happened on the plant overnight.
visual.panelBody: NECTYR reflects how Taypro robots actually work in the field: block-wise plans, seasonal cleaning strategy, and tracker-aware context where GLYDE-X is deployed. Your operators see the signal; Taypro protects the underlying control logic.

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.subtitle: Product-level answers, detailed procedures live in your Taypro customer documentation.
faq.a0: NECTYR is the secure web portal Taypro customers use to monitor and manage their Solar Panel Cleaning Robot fleets across one or many solar sites. It brings together live operational data, schedules, historical cleaning records, performance analytics, and support workflows, so plant and O&M teams can run robotic cleaning with the same rigour they expect from any critical plant system.
faq.q1: How do we get access to NECTYR?
faq.a1: Taypro provisions each client organisation with private access to the production portal. Your account team shares the login link, credentials, and onboarding pack outside this public site. For new deployments, start from our contact form; existing customers can request additional seats, sites, or password help through their Taypro account manager or our support team.
faq.q2: Which Taypro robots work with NECTYR?
faq.a2: NECTYR is the common fleet layer for Taypro autonomous and semi-automatic cleaning robots, including GLYDE (fixed / seasonal tilt), HELYX (pick-and-place), and GLYDE-X (single-axis trackers). The exact screens and commands available depend on your site layout, connectivity, and service package.
faq.q3: Can we schedule cleaning from the portal?
faq.a3: Yes. Client teams can define and review block-wise automation schedules (timers) aligned with your plant's operating rules and weather strategy. Execution still respects on-robot safety interlocks, the portal plans and requests; the fleet executes within engineered limits.
faq.q4: What kind of reporting does NECTYR provide?
faq.a4: NECTYR surfaces site-level and block-wise cleaning coverage, robot battery and execution status, downloadable cleaning reports for audit and internal review, and statistics views for trends over time. Export formats and retention policies are aligned with your enterprise needs during onboarding.
faq.q5: How does NECTYR connect to robots in the field?
faq.a5: Depending on each plant's network design, fleet telemetry and commands travel over secure links such as {connectivity}. Taypro engineers size the gateway and backhaul architecture during deployment so NECTYR stays responsive even on large, multi-block sites.
faq.q6: How do we get help if something looks wrong in the data?
faq.a6: NECTYR includes structured ticketing so your site administrators can raise issues, attach evidence, and track resolution with Taypro's service team. For urgent operational events, your contract may also include direct escalation channels outside the portal.
faq.q7: Is NECTYR a replacement for our plant SCADA?
faq.a7: No. NECTYR is specialised for robotic solar cleaning, schedules, cleaning logs, robot health, and fleet analytics. It complements your existing energy SCADA and work-management tools; Taypro can advise on read-only integrations or export workflows where required.

Common.connectivitySummary: LTE, Wi-Fi, hybrid self-healing RF mesh, LoRa, and LoRaWAN
